description.value:
Source: Ariadne's Book of Legends - Alpha v1.2, p. 78-81.

Level 20

Legendary Points

Starting at 20th level, you gain Legendary Points. Your level determines the number of points you have, as shown in the Legendary Points column of the Legendary Wizard table. You can take the Recover action on your turn to regain all expended Legendary Points. You can spend these points to fuel various features.

Legendary Resistance

Starting at 20th level, you can expend 1 Legendary Point when you fail a saving throw to succeed instead.

Legendary AC

At 20th level, if your AC calculation is lower than your level, you can use your level as your AC instead. If you are using your Legendary AC, you cannot further increase your AC in any way, except by temporary AC increases or magical effects lasting 1 hour or less. Other spells and effects that increase your AC only affect your regular AC.

Cantrips

Prepared

— Spell Slots Per Spell Level — 1st 2nd 3rd 4th 5th 6th 7th 8th 9th 10th

Known

Spells

Legendary Arcanisms

Also at 20th level, you gain the ability to make spells you cast more potent and harder to nullify. When you expend a spell slot to cast a wizard spell, you can expend 1 Legendary Point to upcast it as if you had used a spell slot 3 levels higher than the spell slot you expended, up to a 9th-level upcast. This way, a spell cast at 1st level is instead cast at 4th level, and a spell cast at 6th level is instead cast at 9th level.

source.custom: Original en-US text from Alpha v1.2
